title: Jealous Eyes
content:
The next morning, Li Wei woke up early and went to the palace gardens to practice her calligraphy. She had always loved writing, and it was a way for her to calm her nerves.

As she was writing, she heard a voice.

"Lady Li, what a surprise to see you here."

Li Wei looked up to see Lady Chen standing behind her. She was wearing a beautiful blue dress, and her hair was styled in an elegant updo.

"Lady Chen," Li Wei said, standing up and bowing.

Lady Chen walked over and looked at Li Wei's calligraphy.

"Not bad," she said. "But your strokes are too timid. You need more confidence."

She picked up the brush and wrote a character - bold, powerful, and full of strength.

"Like this," Lady Chen said.

Li Wei nodded, impressed by Lady Chen's skill.

"Thank you for the advice," she said.

Lady Chen smiled, but it didn't reach her eyes. "You're welcome. But remember, Lady Li, in the palace, confidence is not enough. You need to be smarter than everyone else."

She walked away, leaving Li Wei standing alone.

***

Later that day, Li Wei was summoned to the Emperor's study.

"Lady Li," the Emperor said, motioning for her to sit. "I've been meaning to talk to you."

"What about, Your Majesty?"

"Your father," the Emperor said. "He was a great general, and I want to honor his memory."

He stood up and walked to a window, looking out at the palace gardens.

"I'm planning to build a memorial in his honor," the Emperor said. "But I need someone to oversee the project. Would you be willing to help?"

Li Wei's eyes widened. "Me?"

"Yes," the Emperor said. "You're his daughter, and I think you would do a good job."

Li Wei nodded. "I would be honored, Your Majesty."

The Emperor smiled. "Good. I knew I could count on you."

***

As Li Wei left the Emperor's study, she noticed Lady Chen watching her from the corner. There was a look of jealousy in her eyes.

Li Wei walked back to her quarters, her mind racing. The Emperor had given her an important task, which meant that she was gaining favor. But this also meant that she was becoming a target.

That night, as Li Wei was getting ready for bed, she noticed something strange. Her tea had a strange taste - bitter, with a hint of something metallic.

She spit it out, her heart racing. Someone had tried to poison her.

She looked around her room, searching for clues. There was nothing out of the ordinary, but she knew that someone had been there.

Li Wei decided not to say anything. If she accused someone without evidence, she would only make enemies. Instead, she would wait and watch.

The next day, Li Wei went to see Lady Wang.

"Lady Wang," Li Wei said, sitting down. "I need your help."

Lady Wang looked at her, her eyes concerned. "What's wrong?"

Li Wei told her about the poisoned tea.

"Oh no," Lady Wang said. "This is serious."

She leaned forward, her voice low. "You need to be careful, Lady Li. There are people in this palace who would do anything to get rid of you."

"Who?"

Lady Wang hesitated. "I can't say for sure. But I would watch Lady Chen and the Empress carefully."

Li Wei nodded. "Thank you, Lady Wang."

As she left Lady Wang's quarters, Li Wei knew that she had to be more careful. The palace was full of danger, and she was just beginning to understand how deep it went.

But she was not afraid. She had faced danger before, and she would face it again.

And this time, she would win.
